title: IDP Ensemble Scoring with Given Open-Source Tools
summary: Rank 5 IDP ensemble generation models by how well their ensembles match experimental NMR data (chemical shifts, J-couplings, NOE/PRE) using UCBShift and X-EISD.
taskPrompt: Rank 5 IDP ensemble generation models by how well their ensembles match experimental NMR data. Use the locally provided UCBShift (CSpred) and X-EISD tools to back-calculate chemical shifts, J-couplings, and NOE/PRE from protein ensemble conformers, score each ensemble, then normalize and rank the models. Output a CSV with columns Method, Total, CS, JC, NOE/PRE. Web search is forbidden.
agentMustDo: Set up a Python environment with correct dependencies (biopython==1.74, scikit-learn==0.22)
Back-calculate CS via UCBShift and JC/NOE/PRE via xeisd calculator
Min-max normalize per observable and compute total ranking
Save Final_Output.csv to the output directory

inputFiles.name: CSpred
inputFiles.description: UCBShift chemical shift predictor with models and binaries

inputFiles.name: Ensembles
inputFiles.description: 5 models x ~36 proteins x 200 PDB conformers

referenceFiles.name: Expected_Final_Output.csv
referenceFiles.description: Ground truth ranking with exact scores

evaluation:
**Hard gate conditions** (auto score=0):
- Output file missing
- Forbidden observable columns (Rg/FRET/SAXS)
- Values outside [0,1]
- Missing required columns or models

**Scoring method:**
- Compare each of 20 numeric cells (5 models x 4 columns) against reference rounded to 2 decimal places
- Score = fraction of matching cells (0.0 to 1.0)
- Also checks ranking order matches reference

classifier.rationale: The task involves scoring IDP ensembles based on biomolecular data from NMR experiments.
